title: viola tricolor var. ruralis
intro: Viola tricolor var. ruralis, also known as heartsease or wild pansy, is a herbaceous perennial plant belonging to the viola family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing anxiety and stress, improving sleep quality, and providing relief from skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Viola tricolor var. ruralis can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is often used to promote overall well-being and calmness.
